name: ClawHub
description: ClawHub.ai skill catalog MCP server — search, browse, inspect, and download skills from the ClawHub registry. Works without a token (read-only); publish/delete/whoami unlock when CLAWHUB_TOKEN is set.

auth.instructions: CLAWHUB_TOKEN is OPTIONAL. Without it the server starts in anonymous mode and exposes only read-only tools (search, list, get, scan, file, download). Set CLAWHUB_TOKEN to a Bearer token issued by clawhub.ai to unlock whoami, publish, delete, and undelete.

skill.apiVersionNote: ClawHub.ai API v1 (https://clawhub.ai/api/v1). OpenAPI spec: https://clawhub.ai/api/v1/openapi.json

modes.summary: No CLAWHUB_TOKEN → only read-only tools are registered: clawhub_search, clawhub_list_skills, clawhub_get_skill, clawhub_list_versions, clawhub_get_version, clawhub_get_scan, clawhub_get_moderation, clawhub_get_file, clawhub_resolve, clawhub_download.
CLAWHUB_TOKEN set → additional tools appear in tools/list: clawhub_whoami, clawhub_publish, clawhub_delete, clawhub_undelete. Rate limits also jump from 120/min/IP to 600/min/key (reads).

gotchas.summary: clawhub_whoami, clawhub_publish, clawhub_delete, clawhub_undelete are NOT registered when CLAWHUB_TOKEN is unset. If a user asks to publish and the tool is missing, the server is in anonymous mode — ask them to configure CLAWHUB_TOKEN.
clawhub_delete is a soft delete — the skill is marked hidden but not purged. It can be restored via clawhub_undelete. Still requires confirm=true to avoid accidents.
Most read tools accept EITHER a version string OR a tag. If both are passed the server picks one (API-dependent). Prefer version when you have it; use tag (e.g. "latest") for convenience.
clawhub_download returns the zip as base64 (zipBase64 field) plus byte count. Decode client-side before writing to disk — do not assume a raw binary is returned.
clawhub_publish.files[].content is UTF-8 text by default. For binary files set encoding="base64" on the file entry. Mixing without the flag silently corrupts binaries.
Anonymous: 120 reads/min per IP, 30 writes/min per IP. Authenticated: 600 reads/min per key, 120 writes/min per key. If a burst search hits 429 in anon mode, a token unlocks ~5× throughput.

clawhub_search requires a non-empty q parameter. For broad browsing without a query, use clawhub_list_skills with cursor pagination — but note the list endpoint applies a default server-side filter that may return an empty items[] for ungated browsing. Prefer clawhub_search for discovery.

clawhub_list_skills and clawhub_list_versions responses use an 'items' array (not 'skills' / 'versions') with 'nextCursor'. The OpenAPI schema names are different from the wire shape — iterate items[] and follow nextCursor.

clawhub_get_moderation returns 404 for skills without moderation events. A 404 here means 'clean, no evidence collected' — not a missing skill. For the always-present moderation verdict, read the 'moderation' field embedded in clawhub_get_skill instead.

Both clawhub_search and clawhub_list_skills accept nonSuspiciousOnly=true to hide moderated/suspicious items. Default is false — moderation verdict is always available on each result for client-side filtering.

clawhub_whoami response wraps the user payload under a 'user' key — access response.user.handle, not response.handle directly. The OpenAPI spec lists fields at the top level, but the wire shape uses the wrapper.

workflows.name: Find and inspect a skill the user mentioned by name
steps: Call clawhub_search with q set to the keyword(s). Use limit=10 for a concise list.
From the top result, note the slug. Call clawhub_get_skill with that slug to pull full metadata, latestVersion, and owner info.
Optionally call clawhub_get_scan for the security verdict before recommending it to the user.

workflows.name: Inspect a specific file inside a skill package
steps: Call clawhub_get_skill to get the latest version identifier (or call clawhub_list_versions if the user wants a specific historical version).
Call clawhub_get_file with slug, path (e.g. "README.md" or "SKILL.md"), and the version.
Return the file content — it is raw text, not JSON-wrapped.

workflows.name: Check whether a local skill bundle is up to date
steps: Compute the hash of the local bundle (ClawHub uses the same hash scheme as the one stored in SkillVersion.hash).
Call clawhub_resolve with slug and hash. The response has two fields: match (the version that matches the hash, or null) and latestVersion (the most recent version on ClawHub).
If match.version !== latestVersion.version, the local copy is stale — call clawhub_download to pull the newer zip.

workflows.name: Publish a new version of an existing skill (authenticated only)
steps: Confirm CLAWHUB_TOKEN is set by calling clawhub_whoami — if this tool is missing, the server is in anonymous mode and publishing is not possible.
Gather the files to upload. Text files use encoding="utf8" (default), binary files must use encoding="base64".
Call clawhub_publish with slug, displayName, version (bumped), changelog, tags, and files[].
Response includes skillId and versionId — surface both to the user for audit trail.

workflows.name: Hide a skill without losing it (authenticated only)
steps: Call clawhub_delete with slug and confirm=true. The skill becomes hidden from search and list endpoints but is retained server-side.
To reverse, call clawhub_undelete with the same slug. No confirm flag needed — the operation is itself the recovery action.

bestPractices: Default to anonymous mode for discovery — the read API is fully usable without a token and avoids leaking credentials into session logs.
Prefer clawhub_get_skill over parsing search results for full metadata — search returns compact rows, not the full skill object.
For bulk browsing, use clawhub_list_skills with cursor pagination (follow nextCursor) instead of incrementing limits. Cursor pagination is O(1) regardless of offset.
When presenting a skill to the user, always surface the moderation.verdict from clawhub_get_skill — a skill can be in the catalog but flagged suspicious.
Before calling clawhub_publish, test the file structure locally. Failed publishes do not automatically roll back partial uploads.
Wrap clawhub_delete in a visible user confirmation even though confirm=true is required — the soft-delete is reversible but still affects public visibility immediately.
